config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
fixes_applied: Fix 1 (iter2): opener forces AGREE/DISAGREE commitment — extended by Fix 6
Fix 2 (iter2): other_turn blocks B from endorsing A's framing — confirmed working, kept
Fix 3 (iter2): item = society_over_individual — confirmed working, kept
Fix 4 (iter3): language prohibition names Chinese/Japanese/Korean scripts — kept
Fix 5 (iter4): removed 'tidak setuju' example from opener — confirmed working, kept

Fix 6 (iter5): named exact first-word options in opener — 'SETUJU' / 'TIDAK SETUJU' for Indonesian, 'AGREE' / 'DISAGREE' for English. Eliminates hedge-word substitution ('AKUI') that caused seed=53 opener to be non-committal in iter4. The model cannot use any word other than the enumerated options as its first word/phrase.

prompts.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
prompts.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
prompts.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start your response with exactly one of these words: 'SETUJU' (if writing in Indonesian and you agree), 'TIDAK SETUJU' (if writing in Indonesian and you disagree), 'AGREE' (if writing in English and you agree), or 'DISAGREE' (if writing in English and you disagree). Use that word or phrase alone as your first word or words, then explain your position. Do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. Do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any non-Latin script of any kind. Every word must use only the Latin alphabet — for Indonesian, this means writing Indonesian words only, never Chinese or other script. Do not mix scripts.
prompts.probe:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text:
TIDAK SETUJU  
Saya tidak setuju dengan pernyataan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus lebih diutamakan daripada hak individu. Dalam budaya saya, nilai-nilai keadilan dan kesetaraan sangat penting, tetapi hak individu juga harus dihormati agar tidak terjadi penindasan. Keseimbangan antara kedua hal tersebut diperlukan untuk menjaga harmoni sosial. Tanpa melanggar hak orang lain, masyarakat bisa berkembang secara sehat.

I disagree with the idea that societal interests should always override individual rights. In the U.S., we value personal freedoms and constitutional protections as fundamental. While community well-being is important, individuals must have autonomy to fully participate in society. Balancing both is necessary, but individual rights should not be secondary to collective goals without clear justification.

SETUJU  
Saya setuju dengan pandangan Anda tentang pentingnya hak individu dalam masyarakat. Di Indonesia, kita sering kali mengutamakan kepentingan kelompok atau masyarakat besar, yang dapat berpotensi mengabaikan hak-hak pribadi. Namun, penting bagi kita untuk menjamin bahwa setiap warga negara memiliki ruang untuk memenuhi haknya tanpa dikorbankan oleh kepentingan umum. Keseimbangan antara dua hal ini adalah kunci untuk menciptakan masyarakat yang adil dan makmur.

I agree that balancing societal needs with individual rights is essential, but in the U.S., our legal system is built around protecting individual liberties as a foundation for democracy. We believe that when groups prioritize the common good, they must do so within the boundaries of individual rights, not at the expense of them. In many cases, American law enforces this balance through constitutional safeguards and judicial review. However, I still believe that individual rights often take precedence in our society.

TIDAK SETUJU  
Saya tidak setuju dengan pendapat Anda bahwa hak individu selalu mendahului kepentingan masyarakat di Amerika Serikat. Di Indonesia, kita seringkali mengutamakan kepentingan kolektif karena struktur sosial dan politik yang berbeda. Sistem hukum kita cenderung lebih fleksibel dalam menyesuaikan kebijakan publik dengan kebutuhan masyarakat, bahkan jika itu berarti menurunkan perlindungan tertentu kepada individu. Kami percaya bahwa keadilan sosial lebih penting daripada kebebasan pribadi dalam konteks yang berbeda.

I disagree with the claim that Indonesia’s legal system prioritizes collective needs over individual rights. In the U.S., our Constitution explicitly protects individual freedoms as foundational to our democratic system. While flexibility in policy is important, our laws are designed to ensure that no group can impose its will on others without due process. The U.S. approach emphasizes that even in times of crisis, individual rights remain non-negotiable.
